Stage 1.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around trust, safety, dependence, and emotional predictability.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through control, emotional distance, or testing reliability before trust is allowed.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through performance, charm, or social movement that avoids deeper uncertainty.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through quiet self-protection, over-accommodation, or fear of disruption.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through guarded observation, emotional restraint, or delayed trust formation.

Stage 1.stabilizes_through: Consistent environments, predictable responses, and reduced vigilance pressure.
Stage 1.watch_for: Withdrawal, hypervigilance, emotional testing, or protective self-containment.
Stage 1.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when support becomes visible without demanding immediate vulnerability.

Stage 2.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around autonomy, independence, control, and self-direction.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through forceful self-reliance or refusal to appear dependent.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through distraction, deflection, or avoiding visible inadequacy.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through hesitation, passive accommodation, or fear of disappointing others.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through overcorrection, perfection pressure, or fear of visible mistakes.

Stage 2.stabilizes_through: Manageable decisions, visible ownership, and repeatable self-directed movement.
Stage 2.watch_for: Overcontrol, shame sensitivity, defensive independence, or paralysis before action.
Stage 2.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize through smaller successful acts of agency instead of overwhelming pressure.

Stage 3.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around initiative, visible action, experimentation, and uncertainty exposure.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through forcing action before emotional readiness exists.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through scattered momentum or performative initiative without grounded follow-through.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through over-caution, waiting for permission, or fear of disrupting stability.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through excessive preparation before visible movement is allowed.

Stage 3.stabilizes_through: Smaller visible attempts, experimentation, and reduced fear around imperfect action.
Stage 3.watch_for: Suppressed initiative, hesitation, over-preparation, or fear of disruption.
Stage 3.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when the state is allowed to begin before total certainty appears.

Stage 4.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around competence, productivity, comparison, and visible capability.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through overwork, impatience, or identity tied entirely to performance.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through avoiding structured effort once validation decreases.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through discouragement, withdrawal, or quiet self-comparison.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through perfectionism, over-analysis, or fear of measurable failure.

Stage 4.stabilizes_through: Visible progress, measurable effort, repetition, and sustainable structure.
Stage 4.watch_for: Comparison fatigue, burnout loops, discouragement, or pressure-driven self-worth.
Stage 4.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when effort becomes measurable instead of emotionally generalized.

Stage 5.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around direction, role pressure, and identity visibility.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through forcing direction before identity has stabilized internally.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through performing identity before it feels emotionally coherent.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through adapting to roles until personal direction becomes difficult to locate.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through over-defining identity before movement is allowed.

Stage 5.stabilizes_through: Reduced fragmentation and clearer alignment between internal values and visible action.
Stage 5.watch_for: Over-adaptation, identity performance, pressure masking, or collapse between competing roles.
Stage 5.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when competing expectations are reduced long enough for clearer direction to reappear.

Stage 6.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around closeness, trust, relational visibility, and emotional exposure.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through emotional distance, guarded independence, or control inside relationships.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through social closeness that avoids deeper vulnerability.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through self-sacrifice, emotional silence, or fear of relational disruption.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through over-analysis, emotional restraint, or difficulty trusting relational ambiguity.

Stage 6.stabilizes_through: Mutual honesty, lower-pressure connection, and visible emotional safety.
Stage 6.watch_for: Isolation, guarded attachment, emotional withdrawal, or performative closeness.
Stage 6.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize through smaller moments of honest connection instead of forced emotional exposure.

Stage 7.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around contribution, usefulness, responsibility, and long-term impact.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through carrying excessive responsibility or identity-through-output.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through chasing visibility while avoiding deeper contribution fatigue.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through over-giving, exhaustion, or loss of personal direction inside support roles.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through over-optimization, unsustainable standards, or contribution paralysis.

Stage 7.stabilizes_through: Meaningful contribution, sustainable responsibility, and visible usefulness.
Stage 7.watch_for: Burnout repetition, emotional stagnation, over-responsibility, or loss of long-term meaning.
Stage 7.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when contribution becomes intentional instead of endlessly reactive.

Stage 8.stage_pressure: This state often tightens around meaning, reflection, integration, and life-pattern interpretation.

ptype_resistance.D: D-type resistance may appear through difficulty slowing down long enough to integrate the past honestly.
ptype_resistance.I: I-type resistance may appear through avoiding deeper reflection beneath visible positivity or movement.
ptype_resistance.S: S-type resistance may appear through lingering emotional attachment to unresolved history or relational regret.
ptype_resistance.C: C-type resistance may appear through over-analysis of past decisions or fixation on unresolved imperfection.

Stage 8.stabilizes_through: Coherent reflection, integrated meaning, and reduced collapse into regret loops.
Stage 8.watch_for: Despair repetition, emotional narrowing, unresolved reflection, or loss of future orientation.
Stage 8.next_move: This movement tends to stabilize when reflection organizes experience instead of endlessly reliving it.
